violations.expected_code: # Script should have exactly 21 steps matching test case
violations.explanation: Test case analysis shows 21 test steps. The script appears to have 21 steps (1-21), which matches. However, the precondition setup is not clearly separated with a comment separator before Step 1, making it unclear where preconditions end and test steps begin.

The page additions file defines locators as string selectors in an Elements class pattern, but the test script accesses them as direct properties. According to the mapping report and existing PrintHistoryPage structure, these should be accessed via .elements. namespace when they are string selectors, or as direct Locator properties if defined in __init__. The page additions file shows they are string selectors that need to be converted to Locator objects in __init__.

changes.description: Ensure locators are properly structured for Elements class pattern

fix_details.explanation: The access pattern depends on how PrintHistoryPage is structured. If using direct properties (self.X = page.locator()), access as print_history_page.X. If using Elements container (class Elements with strings), access as page.locator(print_history_page.elements.X). The page additions file needs clarification.

fix_details.find:
# Precondition 4: Pause the plan
            framework_logger.info("Precondition: Pausing subscription plan")
            GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
            GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
            # Navigate to subscription edit page and pause
            page.click("text=Edit")
            page.select_option("select[name='subscription[state]']", "paused")
            page.click("input[type='submit'][value='Save']")
            GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info(page, "Subscription State", "paused", retry=True)
fix_details.replace_with:

            framework_logger.info("Precondition: Pausing subscription plan")
            GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
            GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
            # Use helper method to pause subscription (needs to be created if doesn't exist)
            # GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription(page)
            GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info(page, "Subscription State", "paused", retry=True)

                device_uuid=printer_data.device_uuid

violations.explanation: The page additions file defines locators as standalone variables instead of within an Elements inner class. According to the framework pattern shown in the mapping report and coding standards, locators should be defined within a class Elements: structure so they can be accessed via page_obj.elements.locator_name pattern.

fix_details.required_structure:
class Elements:
    # locator definitions

And in __init__:
    self.locator_name = page.locator(self.elements.locator_name)

fix_details.description: Restructure to show clear class structure with Elements inner class and __init__ method

violations.expected_code: # Locators should be in Elements class
violations.explanation: The fundamental structure issue - locators are defined as module-level variables instead of within a class Elements: structure. This violates the framework's Page Object Model pattern.

fix_details.required_structure:
class PrintHistoryPageAdditions:
    class Elements:
        plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
        billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
        # ... other locators
    
    # Then in PrintHistoryPage.__init__:
    self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)

violations.explanation: The file shows __init__ implementation separately under a comment section. This makes it unclear how to properly merge into the existing PrintHistoryPage class.

fix_details.description: Provide complete class structure showing Elements inner class and __init__ method together

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all Python assert statements with Playwright expect() assertions with explicit timeouts. For color verification, create helper methods. For API data verification in preconditions, assert is acceptable. For UI verification, always use expect().

Replace all inline page.click(), page.select_option(), and element.evaluate() calls with proper helper methods or utility functions. For Rails Admin interactions, use GeminiRAHelper methods. For CSS property checks, use core.utils functions like extract_styles(). For screenshots, use take_element_screenshot() from core.utils.
